title: Qualys Global IT Asset Inventory - Join And Scoping Conventions
applies_to: Qualys asset scoping for VMDR reports
Joining classic VM detections to gateway asset inventory data

confirmed_facts.fact: Global IT Asset Inventory v2 uses the gateway base URL and requires a JWT in the Authorization: Bearer header for API requests.
The main asset search route is POST /rest/2.0/search/am/asset and the single-asset detail route is GET /rest/2.0/get/am/asset.
Qualys documents includeFields and excludeFields parameters for limiting asset payloads returned by Asset Inventory APIs.
Asset Inventory responses expose pagination and incremental-sync fields including count, lastSeenAssetId, and hasMore.
The guide documents assetLastUpdated for filtering records updated on or after a UTC timestamp.
Example asset payloads include assetId, assetUUID, hostId, assetType, address, dnsName, assetName, netbiosName, lastModifiedDate, sensorLastUpdatedDate, operatingSystem data, and tag objects.

join_keys: Preferred: detection ASSET_ID -> asset inventory assetId
Fallback: classic host ID -> asset inventory hostId
Vulnerability metadata enrichment: detection QID -> KnowledgeBase QID
implementation_notes: Use search/am/asset as the main scoping surface for asset sets, especially when reports need grouping by tags, OS families, or other asset attributes not consistently present in classic VM host records.

Use includeFields to keep responses small when only a reporting join payload is needed. For most reporting flows, assetName, address, dnsName, netbiosName, operatingSystem, hostId, and tag are sufficient.
Use assetLastUpdated and lastSeenAssetId for incremental refreshes of the asset dimension instead of re-downloading the entire inventory for every report run.
The search route accepts filter bodies in JSON or XML, so asset set construction can be more expressive than only using classic host-list filters.
caveats: Asset Inventory is a gateway API and has a different auth model from classic VM detection APIs, so the integration must manage both auth styles coherently.
Not every classic host detection response will contain ASSET_ID unless show_asset_id=1 is requested.
Tag coverage and business-impact fields depend on what is populated in the tenant inventory and tagging model.
